frameworks.name: Drift-Diffusion Model (DDM)
frameworks.one_line: Prevalent speed-accuracy model whose prediction the paper challenges in value-based choice.

frameworks.name: cost-of-delay optimization framework
frameworks.one_line: Algorithmic balancing of delay cost against obtained reward utility.

frameworks.name: value-based decision making
frameworks.one_line: Domain in which reaction time depends on absolute utility magnitude.

core_question: Can an optimal decision policy explain why reaction times decrease with higher utility magnitude, contrary to DDM predictions?
key_assumptions: Decision policy trades off cost of delaying rewards against obtained utility
Functional form of delay cost is the critical degree of freedom
Subjects' utilities need not be specified parametrically
manipulations: Magnitude of rewards across choice options
Functional form of the cost-of-delay (additive vs multiplicative discounting)
key_findings: Reaction times decrease with higher absolute utility magnitude, contradicting standard DDM
Empirically observed RT-magnitude pattern becomes optimal under multiplicative discounting of delay
Form-free methodology confirms utility magnitude affects RT without assumptions on utility shape
interpretations: Cost structure of time, specifically multiplicative discounting, rationalizes RT sensitivity to reward magnitude
DDM needs augmentation with an explicit time-cost term to capture value-based RT data
